message:
fix(fcp-oauth): redact OAuth2 token-endpoint error details from Display

Follows the same redaction pattern applied to the fcp-oauth OAuth1
`request_token` / `access_token` paths. The OAuth2 `token()` method
previously decoded `TokenErrorResponse` from the upstream server and
embedded both the `error` code and `error_description` into the
`OAuthError::TokenExchangeFailed` message string. Both fields are
attacker-controlled (the "upstream server" in a token-exchange flow
may be anything the flow was pointed at, including a misconfigured
proxy) and the resulting string hits every Display-consuming path:
tracing logs, anyhow chains, HTTP error responses, etc.

Replace the branch:

    let error: TokenErrorResponse = response.json().unwrap_or_else(|_| TokenErrorResponse {
        error: "unknown_error".to_string(),
        ...
    });
    return Err(OAuthError::TokenExchangeFailed(format!(
        "{}: {}",
        error.error,
        error.error_description.unwrap_or_default()
    )));

with a single call to a helper `token_endpoint_unsuccessful_error(status)`
(added in the supporting changes elsewhere in this file's hunks) that
builds a deterministic message containing only our own HTTP status
code — a value we control — and no response body text at all.

Behaviour:

- Callers still get `OAuthError::TokenExchangeFailed`; the variant is
  unchanged.
- `Display`/`Debug` output of the error no longer contains any
  upstream-supplied string.
- The discarded `TokenErrorResponse` body is no longer decoded, which
  is fine because we were throwing away the structured fields
  anyway — the only user of them was the error message we just
  redacted.

Also captures a pure `cargo fmt` reflow in `validate_oauth2_config`
(multi-line call collapsed to one line) that came along with the
redaction hunks; no behaviour change in that hunk.
